factors.name: US Jobs Data (NFP)
factors.summary: Weaker-than-expected nonfarm payrolls reduce Fed rate-hike odds, typically supporting growth/duration equities. TD Securities notes Fed in 'Goldilocks' scenario; gold rallied on this, reducing real-rate pressure on tech valuations.

factors.name: Real Yields / TIPS
factors.summary: Weak jobs data and easing rate-hike expectations compress real yields, which is structurally bullish for long-duration Nasdaq names. UBS sees current yields as buying opportunity in bonds rather than fade.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings & Guidance
factors.summary: Tesla deliveries hit record with European recovery hope (bullish), but Tesla stock sank 7% on report (worst day in ~1 year), signaling sentiment fragility. Meta AI agent progress slower than expected per Zuckerberg (bearish for AI narrative). Nvidia offers compute swaps for startups (neutral—monetization flexibility).

factors.name: AI Sector Momentum
factors.summary: Meta's AI agents underperforming vs OpenAI expectations, and Cointelegraph noted 'AI sector weakness' alongside recent crypto/risk-off moves. Two-day tech-led selloff cited as concern that AI rally ran ahead of fundamentals.

factors.name: Equities Sector Breadth
factors.summary: Nasdaq closed -0.80% (down 207 points) yesterday despite Dow up 1.14%. Tech underperformance vs cyclicals suggests rotation out of growth; S&P 500 flat signals consolidation, not conviction.

factors.name: China Economic Data
factors.summary: China services PMI eased to 54.1 (vs 54.4 prior) but beat estimates; Vietnam growth jumped to 8.39% and Q2 trade beat. Mixed signals—China resilient but moderating. PBOC set yuan at 6.8047 (weaker vs 6.7830 prior close), potential weakness headwind for US exporters.

factors.name: US Dollar Weakness
factors.summary: USD weakened sharply on soft NFP; GBP/USD near 1.3350, AUD/USD positive for second straight day, NZD/USD toward 0.5700. Weaker dollar structurally supports Nasdaq's large international revenue base.

factors.name: Fed Policy Expectations
factors.summary: UBS and TD Securities both signal Fed on hold or delayed tightening. Warsh quoted as downplaying inflation risk despite hike bets, contradicting hard-line Trump pressure. Rate-cut bets now favored into end-2026.

factors.name: 10Y Treasury Yield
factors.summary: No explicit yield data in news feed, but soft jobs + dovish Fed repricing should push yields lower intraday. Long-duration growth names (MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL) benefit from yield compression.

factors.name: Geopolitical / Energy
factors.summary: Strait of Hormuz flows resume post-Iran ceasefire; WTI ~$68.40 (easing). Supply normalization removes inflation/growth shock risk. Oil down ~2% on month; less tail risk for Fed tightening.

factors.name: Holiday Liquidity / Market Microstructure
factors.summary: US markets closed Friday (Independence Day). Thin holiday trading Thursday drove mixed close; elevated volatility risk on thin flows. Friday close will be very light; gaps/squeezes possible Monday reopening.

factors.name: Private Credit / Credit Stress
factors.summary: Blue Owl and other private credit managers capping redemptions (2nd quarter in a row); $15.6B redemption requests vs $5.9B payouts. Liquidity crunch signals risk-off tail; potential contagion if spreads blow out.

factors.name: Antitrust / Regulation
factors.summary: Supreme Court blocked effort to fire Fed Governor Lisa Cook; Trump allies renewing push to reshape Fed. No direct antitrust news on mega-caps (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, META), but regulatory overhang persists.

factors.name: VIX / Volatility
factors.summary: No explicit VIX data in feed, but tech selloff + thin holiday liquidity suggest elevated vol regime. Gamma risk on mega-cap calls likely in play given recent swings.

factors.name: Crypto Correlation
factors.summary: Bitcoin held $61K after weak jobs data; analysts divided on next move. BTC-Nasdaq correlation still positive (risk-on), but no directional conviction in crypto flow yet.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq is caught in a two-way tug: soft US jobs data (NFP miss) and dovish Fed repricing support duration/growth equities via lower real yields and reduced rate-hike odds. However, the selloff in mega-cap tech (Tesla -7%, Meta AI concerns) and the Nasdaq's -0.80% close Thursday (vs Dow +1.14%) signal investors are rotating into value/cyclicals and doubting AI upside. Private credit stress and holiday-thinned liquidity are additional headwinds. The 4h gain of +0.44% overnight is minor and within noise. Overall sentiment is fragile—macro tailwinds (softer rates, weaker dollar) clash with micro headwinds (execution doubts, credit cracks, sector rotation).
catalysts_ahead: US markets CLOSED Friday (July 4 Independence Day) — next session Monday July 7
No major US economic releases expected Friday; thin holiday trading
Earnings season wrapping: watch for any pre-market guidance revisions over weekend
FOMC meeting and CPI release next week (early July) — high bar for conviction until clarity
Geopolitical: Iran ceasefire negotiations ongoing; any escalation re-prices energy, growth risk
Private credit redemptions tracking into Q3 — monitor Blue Owl, Ares, Apollo for gating announcements
screening.risk_warning: Bullish bias invalidated if: (1) jobless claims reaccelerate or unemployment jumps next week, forcing Fed re-hawking; (2) CPI surprise hot, crushing real-yield bullish case; (3) tech earnings next week disappoint or guide down on AI spending; (4) private credit funds gate more aggressively, triggering contagion sell-off; (5) China data rolls over further, reviving global recession fears. Bearish bias invalidated if: (1) jobs data is retested stronger; (2) Fed cuts sooner/larger than expected; (3) mega-cap earnings smash expectations with robust forward guidance; (4) Strait of Hormuz closes again, oil spikes, and inflation re-accelerates.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2; overnight +0.44% bounce is shallow. Wait for retest of 28900 support with confirmed hold + rejection of lower prices before committing longs. For shorts, resistance at 29600–30000 is where profit-taking becomes material; no conviction on extended downside until breadth and credit conditions clarify post-Friday close and next week's data.
